item.title: Ironically this is exactly how the French voted too.
item.description: My French-speaking Imgurians can confirm, that "Can it" is a direct translation but it is more rude than that in the language, it's akin to stfu.

item.title: A Ferocious Dog
item.description: Gary Paulsen photo By Wyoming Department of Education, CC BY-SA 3.0, https://commons.wikimedia.org/w/index.php?curid=111023264 https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0/ Sauce: Edith Hope Fine, Gary Paulsen: Author and Wilderness Adventurer (Berkeley Heights, NJ: Enslow Publications, Inc., 2000), pp. 38-39. “Gary James Paulsen (May 17, 1939 – October 13, 2021) was an American writer of children's and young adult fiction, best known for coming-of-age stories about the wilderness. He was the author of more than 200 books and wrote more than 200 magazine articles and short stories, and several plays, all primarily for teenagers. He won the Margaret Edwards Award from the American Library Association in 1997 for his lifetime contribution in writing for teens. […] “Bits and pieces of Gary's adolescence can be cobbled together in Guts: The True Stories Behind Hatchet and the Brian Books. In that book, Paulsen discusses how he survived between the ages of twelve and fourteen back in Minnesota. He barely mentions his parents except to say that they were too busy being drunk to stock the refrigerator. He worked several jobs during this time, including setting pins at a bowling alley, delivering newspapers, and working as a farmhand. He bought his own school supplies and a .22 single-shot rifle, which he used to hunt for sustenance. Eventually, he gave up the rifle and manufactured his own bow and arrows, which he used to hunt deer.” https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gary_Paulsen  “Reading for me, is spending time with a friend. A book is a friend. You can never have too many.” — Gary Paulsen “Books make me feel safe. Books make me feel normal.” — Gary Paulsen
item.dc:creator: oukid

item.title: sci-fi shenanigans & epic geekery (part 322)

item.dc:creator: lydecker17

item.title: Meme dump to get you trough Friday #250 (hang in there, weekend is coming soon)
item.description:
1. A new villain arises
2. Fear meeee
3
5. "Goth Air"
6. Instincts
7. Yap
8. Well, that went as planned
9
10. Proof she has actual powers!
11. Willem Dafoe on being fan cast as The Joker
12. Snek life
13. When Miata owners see each other in the wild
14. Good thing she has quick reflexes
15. Thank you for upvoting this dump, if you did you are probably as hot as the hottest woman of 1995 - Tea Leoni! P.S. If this dump helped uplift your mood, please consider checking out my passion project - I'm working on a wacky game, inspired by The Stanley Parable, with lots of humor and wholesome moments. Please wishlist on Steam: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/ Thank you for your kindness and have a great weekend!

item.dc:creator: wolfwinter

item.title: Best of luck to you Romi
item.description: My name’s not Henry, and I’m not sure she’s Romi either, or just some scam program sending this shit out. I know I could ignore them, but part of me enjoys hearing them try to turn it into some weird “funny story how we met” type friendship, so I’ll what? Give them all my money? And another part of me wonders if they are real and being forced into working whatever this scam is. So, best of luck to you “Romi” if you need help, and fuck off if you don’t! https://humantraffickinghotline.org/en/contact
item.dc:creator: vant0037
